prerequisites: A Linear workspace with an account that has access to the issues, teams, and projects you want to sync.

Credentials for one of the two supported auth methods: a personal API key, or an OAuth 2.0 application (client_id, client_secret, and a refresh_token obtained from the OAuth flow). This populates the required credentials config field.
Optionally, a start_date to limit how far back records are synced, and num_workers to control sync concurrency.

authMethods.name: Personal API key
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.api_key secret field. Create a key from Linear's Security & access settings (https://linear.app/settings/account/security). The key is sent in the Authorization header on requests to the GraphQL endpoint at https://api.linear.app/graphql. API-key auth is limited to 5,000 requests per hour per user.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields. Register an OAuth2 application at https://linear.app/settings/api/applications/new to get a client_id and client_secret, then complete the authorization-code flow (authorize at https://linear.app/oauth/authorize, exchange the code at https://api.linear.app/oauth/token). Access tokens are valid for 24 hours and are renewed with the refresh token. Available scopes include read, write, issues:create, comments:create, timeSchedule:write, and admin; the read scope is the default. OAuth auth is limited to 5,000 requests per hour per user.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Linear supports two methods: a personal API key for a single-account setup, or OAuth 2.0 for app-style access. Both authenticate against the GraphQL API at https://api.linear.app/graphql.

setupSteps.title: Option A: Create a personal API key
setupSteps.body: In Linear, open Security & access settings at https://linear.app/settings/account/security and create a personal API key. Copy the key and provide it as credentials.api_key. The connector sends it in the Authorization header on every request.

setupSteps.title: Option B: Create an OAuth 2.0 application
setupSteps.body: Go to https://linear.app/settings/api/applications/new and create an OAuth2 application. Note the client_id and client_secret, then run the authorization-code flow (authorize at https://linear.app/oauth/authorize with response_type=code and your redirect_uri, then exchange the code at https://api.linear.app/oauth/token) to obtain a refresh_token. Provide credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the required credentials field with the values from your chosen method. Optionally set start_date to bound the earliest records to sync and num_workers to tune sync concurrency.

sources.title: Linear GraphQL API (authentication, API key, endpoint)
Linear OAuth 2.0 authentication (client_id, client_secret, scopes, token endpoints)
